content.claim: Finnish state policy maintained exceptionally low pupil minimums for rural schools during the 1980s economic growth period, a reversible political choice not an automatic feature of the comprehensive model, enabling temporary protection of small schools.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Claim is stated but not quantified (what 'exceptionally low' means numerically)
No comparative data on what pupil minimums looked like in other countries at equivalent development stage
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
